plan.reasoning:
SMILES: C(=O)C corresponds to acetaldehyde (ethanal). Atom indices: 0=C (SP2, carbonyl carbon), 1=O (SP2, carbonyl oxygen), 2=C (SP3, methyl carbon). No radical electrons on any atom, so this is a Molecule adsorption. The molecule has no unpaired electrons, ruling out ReactiveSpecies.

For acetaldehyde adsorption on metal surfaces, the most common and energetically favorable binding mode is through the oxygen lone pair (O-down) at an ontop or bridge site, since oxygen has lone pairs available for donation to the metal. The carbonyl oxygen (index 1) is the primary donor atom. On a Zn/Hf alloy surface, Hf is more oxophilic than Zn, so adsorption at an Hf ontop site via the oxygen lone pair is expected to be the strongest initial configuration to test.

Plan: O-down (index 1) adsorption at Hf ontop site. This is the most physically motivated first attempt given Hf's oxophilicity and the availability of O lone pairs. site_type='ontop', surface_binding_atoms=['Hf'], adsorbate_binding_indices=[1].
